ClassificationSet: Leadership in a Gen AI World - Supporting Graphic Classes
Purpose: Classify the kinds of reusable visual props that can be generated for slides and Mural exercises without adding more words to the board.
GlobalRules: No readable words or labels inside generated graphics.
Prefer transparent PNG cutouts for easy placement in Mural.
Use the centaur-world prop language even for abstract Gen AI concepts.
Use teal AI glow sparingly so the workshop still feels mythic, warm, and human-led.
Use objects as metaphors, not explanatory diagrams.

Classes.UseCase: Small icons placed near the Gen AI feeling quadrant: empowered, courageous, disempowered, afraid.
Classes.Composition: One centered object with a strong silhouette, gentle contact shadow, and no surrounding scene.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Lifted Torch
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A bronze-and-leather torch with a warm gold flame and a small teal AI spark inside the flame.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Brave Shield
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A round antique-gold and navy shield with a tiny compass-star boss and a few harmless arrow marks.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Loose Reins
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A pair of slack leather reins tangled around a small dim teal orb, implying loss of agency without darkness.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Lantern at Cave Edge
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A small warm lantern illuminating the edge of an unknown path, nervous but not horror-themed.

Classes.UseCase: Props beside the centaur and reverse-centaur swimlanes to reinforce the difference between AI working for us and us working for AI.
Classes.Composition: One medium-size prop cluster, readable from across a Mural board, with more horizontal space than a simple icon.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Partnership Saddle
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A well-fitted leather saddle with bronze tools, a rolled map, and a small teal crystal clipped safely to the side.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Guiding Compass Reins
SuggestedSubjects.Description: Leather reins arranged around a glowing compass, showing direction chosen by the rider rather than imposed by the tool.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Overloaded Pack
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A sagging travel pack stuffed with scrolls, glowing teal forms, and loose straps, playful rather than bleak.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Misfit Bridle
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A bridle awkwardly wrapped around a stack of glowing AI orbs, suggesting the team is adapting itself to the tool.

Classes.UseCase: Small private-reflection icons for the four desk questions: what do you know, what do you need to know, where are you at, where are you going.
Classes.Composition: One symbolic object or tight pair of objects. No text, no labels, no answer content.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Known Scroll
SuggestedSubjects.Description: An unfurled parchment scroll weighted by a horseshoe, with tiny decorative marks and a soft teal constellation thread.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Open Spyglass
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A brass spyglass aimed upward with a small teal glint in the lens, paired with a folded blank map.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: You-Are-Here Cairn
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A small stack of trail stones with a compass needle laid across it and hoofprints nearby.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Future Path Marker
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A forked mountain path marker with two blank pennants, a warm sunrise glow, and a subtle teal line leading forward.

Classes.UseCase: Props around the final group target exercise: how could leaders engage direct reports with the curiosity framework.
Classes.Composition: Circular or radial objects that can sit near a target without competing with sticky notes.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Compass Target
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A circular target blended with a compass rose, antique-gold rings, navy tick marks, and no numbers.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Conversation Campfire
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A small warm campfire ring with three tiny blank parchment cards around it, suggesting team conversation.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Curiosity Arrows
SuggestedSubjects.Description: Four friendly arrows with feather fletching circling inward toward a soft teal question-shaped glow, but no actual question mark text if avoidable.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Mentor Laurel
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A small laurel wreath tied with leather cord and a tiny compass-star charm.

Classes.UseCase: Tiny decorative props that can sit in board corners, beside sticky clusters, or between exercises.
Classes.Composition: Very simple, low-detail, visible at 80-150 px.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Hoofprint Pair
SuggestedSubjects.Description: Two soft brown hoofprints with a tiny antique-gold sparkle.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Blank Pennant
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A small blank navy-and-gold pennant on a wooden pole, no symbol or words.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Bronze Star Pin
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A small compass-star pushpin in antique gold.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Teal Spark
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A tiny contained teal spark with two or three warm gold motes, not a neon burst.

Classes.UseCase: Subtle decorative separators for slide corners, Mural section borders, or transitions.
Classes.Composition: Thin line ornaments inspired by the title art: compass stars, curved gold lines, small navy marks.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Compass Flourish
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A fine antique-gold compass star with two curved horizontal flourishes.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Hoofprint Trail Divider
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A faint trail of tiny hoofprints crossing beneath a thin navy line.

SuggestedSubjects.Name: Mountain Line
SuggestedSubjects.Description: A simple low mountain silhouette line in pale mountain blue with a tiny gold sun.

GenerationBatchSuggestions.Use: One icon per quadrant corner, all same scale and visual weight.
Two partnership/agency props for the centaur lane and two overloaded/misaligned props for the reverse-centaur lane.
One icon per private reflection prompt.
Radial or conversation-oriented icons around the target exercise.
Small decorative elements for empty space, sticky-note clusters, and slide corners.
